key_insight: Bayesian inversion of photoluminescence imaging and drift-diffusion models can localize perovskite solar-cell degradation to specific interfaces versus the bulk, revealing strongly non-uniform aging.
engineering_relevance: This enables device engineers to identify the dominant failure site and target interface passivation strategies, such as amino-silane treatment, to improve long-term stability.
method: Photoluminescence imaging of fully fabricated perovskite solar cells
Drift-diffusion simulations linked to measured optical data
Bayesian inference to generate spatially resolved parameter maps during aging
evidence_signal: The approach was evaluated by tracking parameter-map evolution under accelerated aging at 70 °C under full-spectrum sunlight and using the inferred maps to distinguish hole-contact, electron-contact, and bulk degradation.
watch_item: The inferred degradation maps depend on the fidelity of the drift-diffusion model and imaging assumptions, so mis-specification could bias the identified failure origin.
paper_title: Disentangling the origin of degradation in perovskite solar cells via optical imaging and Bayesian inference
